description: Curated Gene Skin SNP panel for Yeliztli (P3-54). Covers 4 skin pathways (Pigmentation & UV Response, Skin Barrier & Inflammation, Oxidative Stress & Aging, Skin Micronutrients) with 10 curated SNPs across MC1R, FLG, GSTP1, MMP1, VDR, and SOD2 plus GWAS Catalog skin/pigmentation EFO term associations. MC1R uses multi-allele haplotype-aware calling (0/1/2 R alleles). FLG R501X flagged as Insufficient Data due to limited array coverage of FLG loss-of-function variants.

pathways.name: Pigmentation & UV Response
pathways.description: Variants influencing melanin production, skin pigmentation, UV sensitivity, and freckling. MC1R is the primary determinant of red hair / fair skin phenotype and melanoma susceptibility.

CC.effect_summary: No R151C variant. Normal MC1R signaling for eumelanin production at this position.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the R151C 'R' allele. Reduced MC1R signaling shifts melanin production partially toward pheomelanin. Associated with lighter skin, freckling, and modest increase in UV sensitivity.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the R151C 'R' allele. Reduced MC1R signaling shifts melanin production partially toward pheomelanin. Associated with lighter skin, freckling, and modest increase in UV sensitivity.

TT.effect_summary: Homozygous R151C. Strongly reduced MC1R function. Associated with red hair, very fair skin, high freckling, and significantly elevated UV sensitivity and melanoma risk.

snps.recommendation_text: MC1R R151C is a well-established 'R' (strong loss-of-function) allele. Carriers should practice rigorous sun protection including SPF 30+ sunscreen, protective clothing, and avoidance of peak UV hours. Discuss melanoma screening with a dermatologist.

cross_module.note: MC1R R151C is associated with melanoma predisposition. See Cancer module for melanoma risk assessment.

CC.effect_summary: No R160W variant. Normal MC1R function at this position.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the R160W 'R' allele. Reduced eumelanin production. Associated with lighter pigmentation, freckling, and increased UV sensitivity.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the R160W 'R' allele. Reduced eumelanin production. Associated with lighter pigmentation, freckling, and increased UV sensitivity.

TT.effect_summary: Homozygous R160W. Strongly impaired MC1R function. Associated with red/auburn hair, very fair skin, and significantly elevated UV sensitivity and melanoma risk.

snps.recommendation_text: MC1R R160W is a strong loss-of-function ('R') allele. Sun protection is essential for carriers. Discuss skin cancer screening with a healthcare provider.

cross_module.note: MC1R R160W contributes to melanoma predisposition.

GG.effect_summary: No D294H variant. Normal MC1R function at this position.

GC.effect_summary: One copy of the D294H 'R' allele. Partial MC1R loss-of-function. Increased pheomelanin relative to eumelanin. Lighter skin pigmentation and increased UV sensitivity.

CG.effect_summary: One copy of the D294H 'R' allele. Partial MC1R loss-of-function. Increased pheomelanin relative to eumelanin. Lighter skin pigmentation and increased UV sensitivity.

CC.effect_summary: Homozygous D294H. Substantially impaired MC1R signaling. Strong shift toward pheomelanin. Associated with fair skin, red/auburn hair, and significantly elevated melanoma risk.

snps.recommendation_text: MC1R D294H is classified as a strong 'R' allele. Carriers have elevated melanoma risk and should follow dermatologist-recommended screening schedules.

cross_module.note: MC1R D294H is associated with melanoma predisposition.

GG.effect_summary: No R163Q variant. Normal MC1R function at this position.

GA.effect_summary: One copy of the R163Q 'r' (mild) allele. Modestly reduced MC1R signaling. May contribute to lighter pigmentation and mild increase in UV sensitivity, especially when combined with other MC1R variants.

AG.effect_summary: One copy of the R163Q 'r' (mild) allele. Modestly reduced MC1R signaling. May contribute to lighter pigmentation and mild increase in UV sensitivity, especially when combined with other MC1R variants.

AA.effect_summary: Homozygous R163Q. Mildly impaired MC1R function. Associated with modest pigmentation changes. Lower melanoma risk than strong 'R' alleles, but still contributes to cumulative MC1R effect.

snps.recommendation_text: MC1R R163Q is classified as a mild 'r' allele with weaker effect than R151C, R160W, or D294H. Its impact is most significant in combination with other MC1R variants. Standard sun protection is recommended.

pathways.name: Skin Barrier & Inflammation
pathways.description: Variants affecting skin barrier integrity and susceptibility to inflammatory skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis.

GG.effect_summary: No R501X variant detected at rs61816761. This single-site result does not rule out other FLG loss-of-function variants.

GA.effect_summary: One copy of the FLG R501X loss-of-function variant. Reduced filaggrin expression. Associated with impaired skin barrier and increased susceptibility to atopic dermatitis and eczema.

AG.effect_summary: One copy of the FLG R501X loss-of-function variant. Reduced filaggrin expression. Associated with impaired skin barrier and increased susceptibility to atopic dermatitis and eczema.

AA.effect_summary: Homozygous FLG R501X loss-of-function variant. Severely impaired skin barrier. Strong association with atopic dermatitis, ichthyosis vulgaris, and increased allergen penetration.

snps.coverage_note: rs61816761 is the FLG R501X nonsense variant. This single-site result does not assess other FLG loss-of-function alleles such as 2282del4, so a negative result does not rule out FLG loss-of-function. Flagged as Insufficient Data for clinical-grade assessment.
snps.recommendation_text: FLG loss-of-function variants are the strongest genetic risk factor for atopic dermatitis. Carriers benefit from regular emollient use to maintain skin barrier. Note: this result reflects only the assayed R501X site and may not capture all FLG mutations. Discuss comprehensive testing with a dermatologist if clinically relevant.

cross_module.note: FLG barrier defects increase allergen penetration and are linked to the atopic march (eczema → rhinitis → asthma).

pathways.name: Oxidative Stress & Aging
pathways.description: Variants influencing antioxidant defense, collagen metabolism, and skin aging processes including photoaging and wrinkle formation.

AA.effect_summary: Normal GSTP1 enzyme activity (Ile/Ile). Standard glutathione S-transferase detoxification and antioxidant capacity in skin.

AG.effect_summary: One copy of the Val105 variant (Ile/Val). Mildly reduced GSTP1 enzyme activity. Modestly decreased oxidative stress detoxification capacity in skin cells.

GA.effect_summary: One copy of the Val105 variant (Ile/Val). Mildly reduced GSTP1 enzyme activity. Modestly decreased oxidative stress detoxification capacity in skin cells.

GG.effect_summary: Homozygous Val105 (Val/Val). Reduced GSTP1 detoxification activity. Associated with decreased antioxidant defense in skin and potentially accelerated oxidative skin aging.

snps.recommendation_text: GSTP1 Ile105Val affects glutathione-mediated detoxification. Individuals with reduced activity may benefit from dietary antioxidants (vitamins C and E, selenium) and minimizing environmental oxidative stressors (UV, pollution).

indel_polarity.d_token_meaning: deletion — the 1G promoter allele (a single guanine; the shorter -1607 allele, lacking the extra G)
indel_polarity.i_token_meaning: insertion / reference — the 2G promoter allele (two guanines; the longer reference allele whose extra G creates the ETS binding site)
indel_polarity.risk_polarity_note: Unlike the deletion-is-risk indel loci (GJB2/CFTR/APOL1/DHFR), here the INSERTION/reference allele I (2G) is the RISK allele: the extra guanine creates an ETS site that raises MMP1 transcription, so risk_allele=I, ref_allele=D. The D=deletion / I=reference TOKEN convention is unchanged; only which allele carries elevated risk differs.
indel_polarity.vendor_id_convention: 23andMe encodes this indel with literal I/D tokens where I = the 2G (longer, extra-guanine) allele and D = the 1G (shorter) allele; PGP 23andMe files report e.g. II (2G/2G) and DI (1G/2G), passed through unchanged by parser_23andme.py. Ensembl GRCh37 gives allele_string CC/C at 11:102670496-102670497 (MMP1 is minus-strand): the longer CC = 2G = I, the shorter C = 1G = D.
sources: Ensembl REST grch37.rest.ensembl.org/variation/human/rs1799750 — allele_string CC/C at 11:102670496-102670497 (longer CC = 2G = I; shorter C = 1G = D)

Tower et al. 2002 (Biochim Biophys Acta) & 2003 (Eur J Biochem; Breast Cancer Res Treat); Coon et al. 2009 (Matrix Biol); Liu et al. 2011 (Cancer) — the 2G allele inserts a guanine creating an ETS binding site and increases MMP-1 transcription (2G/2G > 1G/1G), establishing 2G (= I, insertion) as the higher-expression risk allele

DD.effect_summary: 1G/1G promoter genotype (D/D, the shorter allele). Lower MMP1 (collagenase-1) transcription. Standard rate of type I collagen degradation in skin.

DI.effect_summary: 1G/2G heterozygous promoter (one 2G insertion allele). Intermediate MMP1 expression. Mildly increased collagen degradation rate compared to 1G/1G.

ID.effect_summary: 1G/2G heterozygous promoter (one 2G insertion allele). Intermediate MMP1 expression. Mildly increased collagen degradation rate compared to 1G/1G.

II.effect_summary: 2G/2G promoter genotype (I/I, the extra-guanine allele). Higher MMP1 transcription via the created ETS binding site. Increased collagenase activity accelerates type I collagen degradation, associated with more pronounced photoaging and wrinkle formation.

snps.coverage_note: The MMP1 -1607 1G/2G polymorphism (rs1799750) is an insertion/deletion: the 2G allele inserts a guanine that creates an ETS binding site and raises MMP1 transcription. 23andMe reports it as I/D indel tokens (I = 2G longer/insertion, D = 1G shorter), which this row keys directly as II/ID/DI/DD (see indel_polarity). Not all arrays genotype this variant, and representation may vary by platform.
snps.recommendation_text: MMP1 2G allele carriers have higher collagenase expression, which may accelerate collagen breakdown and photoaging. Consistent sunscreen use and topical retinoids (under dermatologist guidance) can help counteract collagen degradation.

CC.effect_summary: Ala/Ala genotype. SOD2 mitochondrial targeting signal is efficiently processed. Higher baseline mitochondrial superoxide dismutase activity.

CT.effect_summary: Val/Ala heterozygous. Intermediate SOD2 activity. Modest reduction in mitochondrial antioxidant defense.

TC.effect_summary: Val/Ala heterozygous. Intermediate SOD2 activity. Modest reduction in mitochondrial antioxidant defense.

TT.effect_summary: Val/Val genotype. Reduced mitochondrial SOD2 import efficiency. Lower superoxide dismutase activity. Associated with increased oxidative stress and potentially accelerated skin aging.

snps.recommendation_text: SOD2 Val16Ala affects mitochondrial antioxidant defense. Val/Val carriers may benefit from antioxidant-rich diet and UV protection to mitigate oxidative damage to skin.

pathways.name: Skin Micronutrients
pathways.description: Variants affecting vitamin D receptor signaling and nutrient pathways relevant to skin health, wound healing, and immune modulation in the skin.

GG.effect_summary: VDR FokI FF genotype (rs2228570). Shorter, more transcriptionally active vitamin D receptor isoform.

GA.effect_summary: VDR FokI Ff heterozygous. Intermediate vitamin D receptor activity.

AG.effect_summary: VDR FokI Ff heterozygous. Intermediate vitamin D receptor activity.

AA.effect_summary: VDR FokI ff genotype. Longer, modestly less transcriptionally active vitamin D receptor isoform (start-codon variant). Its association with psoriasis/skin disease is population-specific and inconsistent — the most recent meta-analysis found no FokI-psoriasis association — so no confident skin-risk call is made.

snps.recommendation_text: VDR FokI (rs2228570) is a vitamin D receptor start-codon variant; the ff genotype yields a modestly less active receptor isoform. Its link to psoriasis/skin disease is inconsistent across populations (no association in the most recent meta-analysis), so this is research-grade context, not a deterministic skin-risk call. Adequate vitamin D status supports skin health across genotypes; discuss supplementation with a clinician.

cross_module.note: VDR FokI is shared with the Nutrigenomics vitamin D pathway. See Nutrigenomics module for dietary vitamin D considerations.

GG.effect_summary: VDR BsmI BB genotype (rs1544410), a 3' tagging variant. Reference vitamin D receptor genotype.

GA.effect_summary: VDR BsmI Bb heterozygous. A 3' tagging variant with subtle, inconsistent effects on vitamin D-related skin outcomes.

AG.effect_summary: VDR BsmI Bb heterozygous. A 3' tagging variant with subtle, inconsistent effects on vitamin D-related skin outcomes.

AA.effect_summary: VDR BsmI bb genotype. A 3' tagging variant (in LD with VDR 3'-UTR variants). Its association with psoriasis/skin disease is population-specific and inconsistent — the most recent meta-analysis found no BsmI-psoriasis association — so no confident skin-risk call is made.

snps.recommendation_text: VDR BsmI (rs1544410) is a 3' tagging variant; its association with psoriasis/skin outcomes is population-specific and inconsistent (no association in the most recent meta-analysis), so this is research-grade context. Adequate vitamin D status supports skin health across genotypes.

cross_module.note: VDR BsmI is relevant to the Nutrigenomics vitamin D pathway.

MC1R_multi_allele.description: MC1R multi-allele haplotype-aware calling. MC1R variants are classified as 'R' (strong loss-of-function: R151C, R160W, D294H) or 'r' (mild: R163Q). The cumulative count of strong R alleles across all MC1R positions determines the main UV-sensitivity tier; a called mild R163Q r allele is surfaced separately so it is not collapsed into the no-MC1R-variant baseline.

0_R_alleles.label: Low UV Sensitivity
0_R_alleles.description: No strong MC1R loss-of-function alleles detected. Standard eumelanin production. Baseline melanoma risk.

mild_r_allele.label: Mild MC1R Variant
mild_r_allele.description: No strong MC1R loss-of-function alleles detected, but a mild R163Q ('r') variant was called. This is weaker than the strong R alleles and does not increase the strong-R dosage, but it is not the same as having no MC1R risk variant.

1_R_allele.label: Moderate UV Sensitivity
1_R_allele.description: One strong MC1R loss-of-function allele. Partial shift toward pheomelanin. Increased UV sensitivity and moderately elevated melanoma risk.

2_R_alleles.label: High UV Sensitivity
2_R_alleles.description: Two or more strong MC1R loss-of-function alleles (compound heterozygous or homozygous). Strongly impaired eumelanin production. Very fair skin, high freckling, and significantly elevated melanoma risk. Rigorous sun protection essential.

FLG_R501X_limited_coverage.description: rs61816761 assays FLG R501X, a loss-of-function nonsense variant. This single-site array result does not assess other FLG loss-of-function alleles such as 2282del4, so absence of the R501X risk allele does not rule out FLG loss-of-function mutations. Result is flagged as Insufficient Data for clinical-grade skin barrier assessment.
FLG_R501X_limited_coverage.insufficient_data_reason: Single FLG loss-of-function site; does not capture 2282del4 or other FLG null mutations. Full sequencing required for comprehensive FLG assessment.

scoring_rules.mc1r_aggregate_rule: Count strong R alleles across all MC1R positions. 0 strong R alleles with no mild R163Q call → Low, mild R163Q-only → Mild MC1R Variant, 1 strong R allele → Moderate, 2+ strong R alleles → Elevated.
